summary: Regression + feature test of (1) MindMapCanvas refactor (extracted MapNode + useNodeCrud + useClipboard + useExportHandlers hooks) and (2) ShareDialog universal preview URL (now points to /api/share/:slug/unfurl). Backend: ran /app/backend/tests/test_share_unfurl.py — 5/6 PASS (the 1 failure, TestBaselineOGTags::test_index_html_has_og_and_twitter_tags, is a pre-existing stale assertion checking for an OLD brand string 'mind-mapper · Turn any PDF into a mind-map' in public/index.html — unrelated to this iteration). The 5 PASSING tests cover all required acceptance: (a) GET /api/share/{slug}/unfurl returns 200 + text/html for both real and non-existent slugs, (b) HTML contains <meta property=og:title|og:description|og:image|og:url>, <meta name=twitter:card content=summary_large_image>, <meta http-equiv=refresh>, <link rel=canonical>, (c) cache-control max-age=300 public, (d) x-forwarded-proto=https → og:url + og:image + canonical all rewritten to https://, (e) og:image uses /api/share/{slug}/og.png path, (f) description includes branch count ('2-branch'). Frontend (Studio /app?unlock=mind-mapper67): page loads with the demo map fully rendered (26 nodes including root mm-node-ai-demo-the-republic + nested mm-node-ai-r-1/-1-1/-1-2 etc); right-click on a node opens the full context menu with '+ Child', '+ Sibling', 'Compile to document', 'Add link...', 'Upload file as link...', 'Insert imported bookmark...', 'Add icon...', 'Find this book/paper...', 'Send to Research Assistant', 'Delete map element' AND an inline Map Element Properties panel — my initial 'Add Child' string assertion was a false-negative because the actual button label is '+ Child' (the full menu IS present, confirmed via screenshot); studio-share-btn opens ShareDialog (data-testid=share-dialog) which for an unsigned-in user shows share-dialog-close + share-signin + share-open-card testids — to see the post-create testids share-url/share-copy/share-unfurl-details/share-unfurl-url/share-unfurl-copy/share-preview the user must sign in via Emergent Google OAuth and click share-create (out of scope for unauth test session, but the source code in ShareDialog.jsx lines 297-356 confirms shareUrl is built from /api/share/{slug}/unfurl and directViewerUrl from /share/{slug}, with collapsible <details data-testid=share-unfurl-details> wrapping the direct link); Export PNG button (mm-tb-export-png) triggers a real file download ('Demo_The_Republic_Plato_.png' captured by playwright expect_download); 150 unique data-testids found across the Studio canvas (mindmap-canvas, mm-top-toolbar, mm-tb-zoom-in/out/level, mm-tb-export-png/svg/md/pdf/json, mm-tb-screenshot, mm-tb-print, mm-tb-cloud-save, mm-tb-undo/redo, mm-tb-fit, mm-tb-orient-toggle, etc — all wired). One non-blocking REGRESSION found: React 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning fires twice in console after Studio mount — strongly suggests a setState-inside-useEffect loop introduced by the new MapNode/useNodeCrud/useClipboard hook plumbing (the warning was NOT present in iteration_75 testing). App still functions but this should be investigated.

minor.issue: test_share_unfurl.py::TestBaselineOGTags::test_index_html_has_og_and_twitter_tags is asserting an OLD brand string ('mind-mapper · Turn any PDF into a mind-map') that no longer exists in public/index.html. og:title meta tag IS present, just with a different string. This is a stale test assertion — either update the test to assert the new brand string or update public/index.html if the old string is still desired.

ui_bugs.issue: React console warning 'Maximum update depth exceeded. This can happen when a component calls setState inside useEffect, but useEffect either doesn't have a dependency array, or one of the dependencies changes on every render.' fires twice on Studio mount. App still functions but indicates a render loop in the new hook wiring — most likely a callback prop being recreated on every render (no useCallback) and used in a child useEffect dependency array. Recommend wrapping the addChild/addSibling/removeNode/updateNode/copy/paste callbacks in useCallback and stabilising any object/array deps passed from MindMapCanvas to MapNode.

action_items: Investigate 'Maximum update depth exceeded' React warning in MindMapCanvas refactor — likely stems from a non-memoised callback in renderNode() (lines ~852-940) being passed to MapNode and triggering a useEffect inside the child to re-fire on every parent render. Wrap addChild/addSibling/removeNode/updateNode/onCopy/onPaste in useCallback with stable deps.

Update or remove stale test assertion in /app/backend/tests/test_share_unfurl.py::TestBaselineOGTags (line 121) — the asserted brand string 'mind-mapper · Turn any PDF into a mind-map' is no longer in public/index.html.

If a signed-in regression for the new ShareDialog universal-URL UI is required, an authenticated session (Emergent Google OAuth) needs to be supplied — current testing only covered the source-code wiring + the unsigned-in dialog state.
critical_code_review_comments: ShareDialog.jsx line 68: shareUrl = `${window.location.origin}/api/share/${slug}/unfurl` — correctly assembles the universal preview URL using window.origin (dynamically picks up https://marvex.app or the preview URL); directViewerUrl = `${window.location.origin}/share/${slug}` for the collapsible direct link.

ShareDialog.jsx lines 318-349: <details data-testid=share-unfurl-details> with summary 'Direct viewer link (no preview card)' wraps share-unfurl-url + share-unfurl-copy — exactly matches review_request acceptance criteria.

ShareDialog.jsx lines 294-310: primary share-url input + share-copy button copies the unfurl URL (via handleCopy → shareUrl). Confirmed by reading code that handleCopy uses shareUrl, not directViewerUrl.
Backend /api/share/:slug/unfurl already passes all 5 dedicated unit tests including https proto rewrites and 200-fallback for nonexistent slugs.

REGRESSION RISK: The MindMapCanvas refactor introduced a 'Maximum update depth exceeded' React warning. Strong indicator of a useEffect loop. While the app appears to keep functioning, this can degrade performance and may eventually trigger a hard React error in production. Highest-priority code review item.

Suggest adding a unit/integration test that asserts the React tree mounts WITHOUT firing the 'Maximum update depth' warning (e.g., by spying on console.error during a test render). This would catch hook-wiring regressions early.

success_rate.backend: 83% (5/6 in test_share_unfurl.py — the 1 failure is a stale unrelated assertion). 100% (5/5) for the new unfurl endpoint coverage.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on testable scope: Studio loads, demo map renders (26 nodes), right-click context menu opens with full options, ShareDialog opens (unsigned-in state correctly shows share-signin), Export PNG triggers real download. Signed-in share-url UX path verified via source code (cannot be exercised live without Google OAuth).

test_credentials: ?unlock=mind-mapper67 only. Signed-in ShareDialog flow requires Emergent Google OAuth.
seed_data_creation: None. test_share_unfurl.py creates ephemeral shares via TestClient + dependency_overrides[current_user_dep] and deletes them in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The MindMapCanvas refactor (MapNode + useNodeCrud + useClipboard + useExportHandlers) introduced a 'Maximum update depth exceeded' React warning — please re-test for this regression after the main agent fixes the useCallback memoisation. 150 Studio testids are catalogued in this iteration for reference. Real node testids on demo are mm-node-ai-demo-the-republic (root) + mm-node-ai-r-{n}-{m} (children) for The Republic demo, mm-node-ai-pp-* for Pride and Prejudice. Both maps cycle on each Studio load. Tour-skip data-testid='tour-skip' must be clicked before any node interaction or the tour-overlay blocks pointer events. /api/share/:slug/unfurl backend is fully verified — focus future testing on the signed-in UI flow if Google OAuth becomes available in this env.
rca of the issue: REACT WARNING RCA: 'Maximum update depth exceeded' is fired when a useEffect inside a child component calls setState whose dep array contains a value that is recreated on every parent render (e.g., an inline arrow function or a freshly-built object). The MindMapCanvas refactor extracted MapNode and pushed CRUD callbacks down through props; if those callbacks are re-created in renderNode() (lines ~852-940 of the new MindMapCanvas.jsx) without useCallback, every state change in the parent triggers a re-render of every MapNode, whose useEffect dependency on the (now-different) callback re-fires, calling setState, looping. Reproduction: load /app?unlock=mind-mapper67 in Chrome → open DevTools console → wait 2s after Studio mount → see the warning fire twice. Mitigation: wrap addChild/addSibling/removeNode/updateNode/onCopy/onPaste/onDoubleClick/onSelect callbacks in useCallback with primitive-only deps in MindMapCanvas, and ensure useNodeCrud/useClipboard return memoised handlers (they should already, but verify).
